title: Watermelon Pieces – golden, golden jewels

seo.title: Watermelon Pieces - golden , golden jewels - Marinda Kook

html:
I was given a makataan watermelon as a present. It lay there on the back table peering at me. I peered back at it. It asked to be slaughtered, so I slaughtered it.

  • Cut your watermelon into rings of about 5 cm.
  • cut out the inner flesh and throw it away.
  • cut off the green rind and throw it away – or give it to the chickens.
  • now cut your clean rinds into 5 cm pieces (chunks)
  • prick the pieces all over. ALL OVER!
  • buy lime from the pharmacy.
  • put your watermelon pieces in a bowl and sprinkle 5 tablespoons of lime over your pieces.
  • fill your bowl with water until everything is covered.
  • leave overnight.
  • the next morning rinse yourwatermelon pieces well.

 

 

 

NOW:

  • first weigh your watermelon. Mine weighed 2 kg so my measurements are based on 2 kg.
  • you need 2 pots. Fill the first pot with water and bring it to the boil. Place your watermelon pieces in the pot and cook for 20 minutes until the watermelon starts to become translucent.
  • in your 2nd pot pour 3 kg sugar, 4 litres water, 2 pieces of bruised ginger, the juice of 2 lemons and the lemon halves. Let it boil until the sugar has dissolved.
  • now scoop your watermelon pieces from the boiling water into the boiling syrup. Cook it for at least 3 hours until the watermelon is golden brown.

NOW:

  • sterilise your jars by washing them on a cycle in your dishwasher or by boiling them in boiling water on the stove for 10 minutes. Remember to sterilise the lids too.
  • spoon the golden brown watermelon pieces into your jars and top them up with the syrup.
  • screw the lids on tightly.

Enjoy your preserve with cheese or freshly baked bread.
